strategy: Trap with mass/all-enemies spells (Mass Feint) — single traps get Cleansed off by Ms. Felicity.
Don't cast Auras: Kenny Rooster destroys any aura with a 300 Fire hit.
Attacking N. Bison makes him retaliate for 20% of your max health, so keep healed and plan to burst rather than trade pokes.
N. Bison confuses every wizard on round 1 and every 7 rounds; hit hard in the clear windows and stun him (he's stunable) when you can.
With only 2,595 health and low-health minions, stack blades and drop a blade-boosted AoE to clear the whole gauntlet at once; attack with Storm (+20%).
cheatNotes: Join late and N. Bison confuses you for two rounds — enter on time.
Round 1 and every 7th round he confuses every wizard for two rounds (random targeting) — don't rely on precise targeting during confusion.
Attacking N. Bison makes him retaliate for 20% of your max health — expect chip damage every time you hit him; keep healed and burst fast.
Ms. Felicity Disarms a wizard who receives a blade from a teammate — funnel blades carefully or use self-buffs.
A single Trap gets Cleansed off its target by Ms. Felicity — use mass or all-enemies traps instead.
Mass Feint and other all-enemies traps don't trigger the Cleanse — use those.
Daniel hands the team Free Sample and Snake Oil cards — junk that clogs your hand; discard them.
Casting any Aura makes Kenny destroy it with a 300 Fire hit — don't bother with Auras in this fight.
